title: Bulbophyllum niveum
taxonomy: Bulbophyllum niveum is a species of orchid. It belongs to the family Orchidaceae. This species is further classified under the subfamily Epidendroideae and tribe Dendrobieae. Bulbophyllum niveum is a genus of orchids, with B. niveum being one of the many species within this genus. The classification of Bulbophyllum niveum reflects its evolutionary history and relationships with other orchid species.

intro: Bulbophyllum niveum, also known as the Snowy Bulbophyllum, is a species of orchid that belongs to the family Orchidaceae. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, cough, and respiratory problems, due to its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Bulbophyllum niveum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. In herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared into teas, infusions, or extracts, and is commonly used to treat respiratory infections and to relieve symptoms of bronchitis and pneumonia.
